question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 17?

fragility_points: Binance-specific data or candle-labeling anomaly near the operative minute.
key_assumptions: BTC stays in roughly its current regime and does not suffer a >6% drawdown before settlement.
Binance remains the stable operative settlement source for the relevant 1-minute candle.
main_logical_chain: Market implies 99.05% Yes.
Named settlement venue is Binance BTC/USDT, so direct Binance data is the right primary check.
Current Binance price sits materially above 70000 with less than a day to go.
That makes Yes highly likely under ordinary conditions.
But one-day crypto downside tails are real, so 99% is slightly too aggressive; 96% is more defensible.
payload.main_thesis: BTC/USDT on Binance is already comfortably above 70000, so Yes is very likely by Apr. 17 noon ET, but not quite as certain as the market's 99% price implies.
payload.source_quality_view: Source quality is strong enough for a medium-difficulty case: contract mechanics came from the Polymarket rules page and market-state verification came from direct Binance API checks plus an additional Binance verification pass.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can still fall 6-7% in under a day, which is enough to flip this single-minute settlement market to No.
There is residual timestamp / operational edge-case risk in single-minute exchange-settlement contracts.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance API checks showed BTC/USDT around 74.7k-74.9k, well above the threshold.
Additional 24h Binance stats showed the sampled low still above 73.5k.
Contract mechanics are explicit about Binance, BTC/USDT, 12:00 ET, and final Close > 70000.
payload.timing_relevance: This is a date-specific one-minute settlement market for Apr. 17, 2026 at 12:00 ET, so short-horizon volatility and exact candle timing matter more than broader BTC narratives.
unresolved_ambiguities: Small residual ambiguity remains around practical ET-to-candle mapping until the final settlement minute arrives.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would cut the probability if later Binance checks showed BTC much closer to 70000, if volatility expanded sharply, or if credible evidence emerged of a Binance settlement-minute data anomaly.
